draft.introduction: The three young men in the fiery furnace refuse to worship Nebuchadnezzar’s golden image and are thrown into the flames. A fourth figure appears in the fire “like a son of the gods.”

translation.text: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego answered and said to the king, “O Nebuchadnezzar, we have no need to answer you in this matter. If this be so, our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace. But if not, be it known to you, O king, that we will not serve your gods.”

Then King Nebuchadnezzar was astonished and rose up in haste. He said to his counselors, “Did we not cast three men bound into the fire?” They answered, “True, O king.” He answered, “But I see four men unbound, walking in the midst of the fire, and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods.”
translation.original_text: καὶ ἡ ὅρασις τοῦ τετάρτου ὁμοία υἱῷ θεοῦ

translator_notes.note: The Aramaic bar elahin (“son of the gods”) was read christologically by the Fathers as a pre-incarnate appearance of the Son. The pagan king recognizes the divine presence without understanding it—a pattern the Fathers saw throughout the OT.
